titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/141

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=413 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:06:35.762427+00 | translation moa=414 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:06:37.856219+00

span.cl:
ntes quam futuri quod Ego Simon de Lindess’ dedi . Concessi . et hac Carta mea confirmaui Patricio homini meo et
. . . pro homagio . et seruicio suo in terra mea de Molle quam ex dono matris mee habui . sex acras terre . et unam acram prati
et unam acram et dimidiam terre in tofto et crofto et unam acram prati illi terre proximam iuxta Erdeburesburne . et subchestres
. . . ras . et super Selestede ade . duas acras et dimidiam . Tenend’ . sibi et heredibus suis de me et heredibus meis in feodo et hereditate
libere et quiete . plenarie et honorifice cum omnibus etiam communibus a[i]siamentis terre mee et communibus aisiamentis que homines mei in terra matris mee
possunt et habere debent . Reddendo mihi et heredibus meis . unam libram Cimini . uel tres denarios ad festum sancti . Iacobi . pro omnibus serui
ciis ad me uel ad heredes meos pertinentibus . et faciendo forinsecum seruicium quantum pertinet ad tantum terre in terra mea . volo itaque
. . . ut mihi et heredibus meis pro forisfactio suo det quatuor denarios . et pro auxilio suo mihi et heredibus meis . sex denarios .

His testibus Petro de valoniis . Roberto de Raperl’ . Ada de waleuhop’ . Ada de whitton’ . et Gileberto filio suo . Waltero de
. . . Ric’ Scoto . Meruino de Molle . Ric’ . clerico . et multis aliis .

p.cl: Let present and future know that I, Simon of Lindsay, have given, granted, and by this my charter made firm to Patrick my man and [in return for] his homage and service in my land at Mow, which he has had from the gift of my mother, six acres of land, and one acre of meadow, and one and a half acres of land in toft and croft, and one acre of meadow of the the land nearest ‘Erdeburesburne’, and ‘Subchestres[?burne]’ . ?two acres . and upon ‘Selestede Adam’ two and a half acres; for him and his heirs to hold of me and my heirs in feu and heritage freely and quitely, fully and honourably, also with all common easements of my land and common easements which my men in my mother’s land [can] and ought to have; by paying me and my heirs one pound of cumin or three pence at the feast of St James in return for all services pertaining to me or my heirs, and by doing such forinsec service on my land as pertains to such an amount of land; I therefore will that he should give four pence [to me and] my heirs for his fine, and six pence for his aid.
